REPLY BRIEF RE: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 15034272) FILED BY DEFENDANT FONTANA WEST APARTMENT  CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHALL FILE ITS PROPOSED SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. AS A CONDITION OF THE COURT GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR BIFURCATION OF THE COMPLAINT AND THIS CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED FOR PURPOSES OF TRIAL ONLY. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS W. CHAFFEE of Morris, Polich & Purdy, LLP, Tel No. (415) 984-8500 appearing for Plaintiffs Raoul D. Kennedy and Martha Nolte Kennedy, Trustees of the Raoul and MArtha Kennedy Revocable Trust, Dated July 11, 2007;
Attys. JOHN L. FITZGERALD and KACIE L. MANISCO of Freeman, Mathis & Gary, LLP, Tel No. (415) 394-9500 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Fontana West Apartment Corporation.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ordered as follows: CROSS-COMPLAINANT FONTANA WEST APARTMENT CORPORATION Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. Cross-complainant shall file its proposed Second Amended Cross-Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. It is the established policy of the state to liberally permit amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceedings. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) Further, "if the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Cross-complainant is seeking leave to amend approximately three months after filing the original cross-complaint. Discovery in the case is in the preliminary stages. No trial date is set. Thus, the motion to amend is timely made and Plaintiffs cannot establish prejudice. As a condition of the Court granting leave to amend, Plaintiffs' request for bifurcation of the Complaint and this Cross-Complaint is granted for purposes of trial only. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
